title: Andrej Karpathy - Wikipedia
text:
Czechoslovak-born AI researcher (born 1986)

**Andrej Karpathy** (born 23 October 1986 <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> ) is a Slovak-Canadian AI researcher <sup>[[ 4 ]](:3-4)</sup> , who co-founded and formerly worked at OpenAI , <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup> where he specialized in deep learning and computer vision . <sup>[[ 8 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 9 ]](.com-9)</sup> <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> He served as the director of artificial intelligence and Autopilot Vision at Tesla . In 2024 he founded Eureka Labs, an AI education platform, whose first course will be LLM101n: Let's Build A Storyteller. <sup>[[ 10 ]]()</sup>

## Education and early life

 [edit ]

Karpathy was born in Bratislava , Czechoslovakia (now Slovakia), <sup>[[ 11 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 12 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 13 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 14 ]]()</sup> and moved with his family to Toronto when he was 15. <sup>[[ 4 ]](:3-4)</sup> He completed his Computer Science and Physics bachelor's degrees at University of Toronto in 2009 <sup>[[ 15 ]](:0-15)</sup> and his master's degree at University of British Columbia in 2011, <sup>[[ 15 ]](:0-15)</sup> where he worked on physically simulated figures (for example, a simulated runner or a simulated person in a crowd) with his adviser Michiel van de Panne.

In 2006, Karpathy began posting videos on YouTube on his channel, _badmephisto_ . He garnered fame by posting Rubik's cube tutorials which have been used by famous speedcubers such as Feliks Zemdegs . <sup>[[ 16 ]]()</sup> The channel has over 9 million views as of June 2025.

Karpathy received a PhD from Stanford University in 2015 under the supervision of Fei-Fei Li , focusing on the intersection of natural language processing and computer vision, and deep learning models suited for this task. <sup>[[ 17 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 18 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 19 ]]()</sup>

## Career and research

 [edit ]

He authored and was the primary instructor of the first deep learning course at Stanford, _CS 231n: Convolutional Neural Networks for Visual Recognition_ . <sup>[[ 20 ]]()</sup> The course became one of the largest classes at Stanford, growing from 150 students in 2015 to 750 in 2017. <sup>[[ 21 ]](:1-21)</sup>

Karpathy is a founding member of the artificial intelligence research group OpenAI , <sup>[[ 22 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 23 ]]()</sup> where he was a research scientist from 2015 to 2017. <sup>[[ 21 ]](:1-21)</sup> In June 2017 he became Tesla's director of artificial intelligence and reported to Elon Musk . <sup>[[ 24 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 9 ]](.com-9)</sup> <sup>[[ 25 ]]()</sup> He was named one of MIT Technology Review 's Innovators Under 35 for 2020. <sup>[[ 26 ]]()</sup> After taking a several-months-long sabbatical from Tesla, he announced he was leaving the company in July 2022. <sup>[[ 27 ]]()</sup> As of February 2023, he makes YouTube videos on how to create artificial neural networks . <sup>[[ 28 ]]()</sup>

On February 9, 2023, Karpathy announced he was returning to OpenAI. <sup>[[ 29 ]]()</sup>

A year later on February 13, 2024, an OpenAI spokesperson confirmed that Karpathy had left OpenAI. <sup>[[ 30 ]]()</sup> In the same year, he was named one of Time Magazine") 's 100 Most Influential People in AI . <sup>[[ 31 ]]()</sup>

On July 16, 2024, Karpathy announced on his X account that he started a new AI education company called Eureka Labs. <sup>[[ 32 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 33 ]](:02-33)</sup> Their first product was the AI course, LLM101n. <sup>[[ 34 ]]()</sup> He also has a broader educational effort, the "Zero to Hero" series on LLM fundamentals. <sup>[[ 35 ]]()</sup> The company also advocates for AI teaching assistants, a concept which has been criticized due to data privacy concerns and the removal of personal connection between teacher and student. <sup>[[ 36 ]]()</sup>

In February 2025, Karpathy coined the term vibe coding to describe how AI tools allow hobbyists to construct apps and websites just by typing prompts. <sup>[[ 37 ]](:2-37)</sup>
